summary: Ran 28 pytest cases against the DocuMind backend (catalog, completeness, generation lifecycle, versions, duplicate, patch, list filters, pipeline, interview, reviewer, templates, settings, export, improve, negative). 12 passed and 16 failed — every failure traces back to a single root cause: the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY monthly budget is exhausted ('Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 2.439501, Max budget: 2.4'). Once the LLM key is refreshed, the failing tests should pass since the backend logic, persistence, routing, and 502 error-handling are otherwise wired correctly. Front-end smoke check confirmed the Dashboard loads and all 6 category data-testids (category-ba..category-hr) render.

critical.issue: All AI-backed endpoints fail with HTTP 502 because Emergent Universal LLM key monthly budget is exceeded ($2.4395 / $2.4). Backend logs show repeated `litellm.BadRequestError: Budget has been exceeded`. Long retries cause requests to also hit Cloudflare 100s ingress timeout (returning a Cloudflare 502 HTML page).

minor.issue: AI calls that succeed can take >100s, which exceeds Cloudflare/ingress timeout in this preview environment. Consider streaming responses or a polling/job-based pattern for long generations to avoid 5xx at the edge.

_next_version logic resets to 1.0 if the parent has its own version like 1.5 and there are no children — `base_versions = [parent_doc['version_number']]` then `+ ['1.0']` is appended which can pick the wrong max for non-1.x parents. Edge case only.

Inserts `_id` is not stripped from `doc` BEFORE merge — but since it was fetched with `{_id:0}` it's safe. However the duplicate also copies `inputs` and `content` by reference; for very large docs consider deep copy.

ui_bugs.issue: Generator cards rendered but have no `data-testid="generator-card-*"` attribute. Only `data-testid="category-*"` group containers are tagged. Will impair Playwright targeting in subsequent tests.

integration_issues.flow: Any flow that calls AI (Generator, Reviewer, Interview generate, Pipeline, Improve, Regenerate, New version)
integration_issues.issue: All these UI flows will fail with toast/500 because the backend AI calls are blocked by the LLM budget exhaustion. UI itself is not at fault.

action_items: TOP PRIORITY: Refresh / increase the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget cap (currently exceeded at $2.44/$2.40). All AI workflows are blocked until then.
After budget refresh, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_documind.py — expectation is the 16 failing tests will pass, since the backend code path is wired correctly (12/28 already pass without LLM).
Add `data-testid="generator-card-<key>"` to each generator card on the Dashboard so the front-end test suite can target the 29 cards individually.

Consider switching long AI endpoints (/api/generate, /api/review/upload, /api/pipeline/generate) to an async job pattern (return 202 + poll job_id) to avoid Cloudflare 100s ingress timeout in production.
Verify _next_version handles non-1.x parents correctly (minor edge case).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~670 lines and growing — fine for now but extract routers (documents.py, interview.py, exports.py, templates.py) when adding more.

_persist_new_document mixes parent/version resolution with persistence — readable but should ensure `parent_id` resolves to root parent if a child id is passed (currently passes child id from `versions` route, which sets `parent_id = doc.get('parent_id') or doc['id']` correctly — good).
All Mongo reads use {'_id': 0}, so ObjectId serialization is safe ✅.
All endpoints prefixed with /api ✅. CORS configured from env ✅.
ai_engine: ensure JSON robustness — wrapping `_extract_json` should already strip markdown fences. Consider returning HTTP 503 (instead of 502) for budget/rate-limit errors so client can distinguish.

success_rate.backend: 43% (12/28). All 16 failures are LLM-budget caused; non-AI infra at 100%.
success_rate.frontend: Smoke only — Dashboard + all 6 category data-testids OK.

test_credentials: Not required — app has no auth.
seed_data_creation: Test data was prefixed with TEST_ and most cleanup steps were not reached due to LLM 502 failures; a few TEST_-prefixed templates/settings may remain in the DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_documind.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` AFTER the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is refreshed. Tests are self-contained; the lifecycle tests share state via class-level attributes (GENERATED_ID, VERSION_ID, DUP_ID). For frontend, add `data-testid=generator-card-<key>` first, then run Playwright flows for Generator (Quick Form + Interview), Reviewer, Templates upload, Settings save, History filters, and DocumentViewer actions (Copy/PDF/DOCX/Edit/Versions/Pipeline).
rca of the issue: Root cause: Emergent Universal LLM key has consumed $2.4395 against a $2.4 ceiling. Reproduction: hit any AI endpoint (/api/generate, /api/review/upload, etc.) — backend logs show `litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 2.439501, Max budget: 2.4` and the FastAPI handler converts this into HTTP 502 (`AI generation failed`). Long retries inside litellm also cause the request to exceed Cloudflare's 100s ingress timeout, producing a Cloudflare 502 HTML page in some tests. Mitigation: refresh / raise the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY monthly budget. No code change required.
